articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Matthijs de Ligt (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for hand ball.
Kevin Mbabu (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card.
Goal!  FC Augsburg 0, FC Bayern München 1. Aleksandar Pavlovic (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from very close range to the top left corner    following a corner.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Mathys Tel replaces Kingsley Coman because of an injury.
Goal!  FC Augsburg 0, FC Bayern München 2. Alphonso Davies (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Leon Goretzka.
First Half ends, FC Augsburg 0, FC Bayern München 2.
Second Half begins FC Augsburg 0, FC Bayern München 2.
Goal!  FC Augsburg 1, FC Bayern München 2. Ermedin Demirovic (FC Augsburg) with an attempt from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Kevin Mbabu with a cross.
Goal!  FC Augsburg 1, FC Bayern München 3. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from the left side of the six yard box to the centre of the goal.Goal awarded following VAR Review.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Dion Beljo replaces Phillip Tietz.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Mads Pedersen replaces Elvis Rexhbeçaj.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Arne Engels replaces Fredrik Jensen.
Aleksandar Pavlovic (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Iago (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Arne Maier replaces Iago.
Leroy Sané (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Sven Michel replaces Rubén Vargas.
Penalty saved. Sven Michel (FC Augsburg)  left footed shot saved  in the bottom right corner.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Eric Choupo-Moting replaces Leroy Sané.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Thomas Müller replaces Jamal Musiala.
Goal!  FC Augsburg 2, FC Bayern München 3. Ermedin Demirovic (FC Augsburg) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Raphaël Guerreiro (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card.
Leon Goretzka (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Lovro Zvonarek replaces Aleksandar Pavlovic because of an injury.
Manuel Neuer (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card.
Second Half ends, FC Augsburg 2, FC Bayern München 3.

article.story:
Injury-hit Bayern Munich battled to a 3-2 victory at Augsburg in the Bavarian derby on Saturday to cut the gap with Bundesliga leaders Bayer Leverkusen to one point.

First half goals from 19-year-old Aleksandar Pavlovic and Canada international Alphonso Davies plus one from the league's top scorer Harry Kane just before the hour took second-placed Bayern to 47 points.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Bayer Leverkusen, who missed many chances and had to settle for a 0-0 draw with visitors Borussia Monchengladbach, are top of the standings on 49.

Bayern, whose winger Kingsley Coman suffered a torn knee ligament and will be sidelined for several weeks, conceded two penalties late in the game with Augsburg converting one of them in stoppage time.

"It was an unusual lineup for us with all the injuries and absences," Bayern head coach Thomas Tuchel said. "We had a bit of luck at the start. We knew it was going to be tough against them. But then we got better and created lots of chances.

"In the end it got a bit turbulent with the two late penalties. Overall I am satisfied with the attitude and the performance of my team."

The hosts got off to a stronger start and also put the ball in the net before a VAR review cancelled out the goal.

Bayern were missing nine players through injury or international duty, including Konrad Laimer, Joshua Kimmich and Dayot Upamecano, who were all injured in the midweek 1-0 home win over Union Berlin.

They still managed to take the lead with teenager Plavlovic turning in the box and drilling home his first league goal in the 23rd minute.

Only a minute later, however, Bayern winger Coman had to be helped off the pitch after what looked like a knee injury, piling more pressure on an already depleted squad.

"FC Bayern will be without Kingsley Coman over the coming weeks after the France international suffered a torn medial collateral ligament in his left knee in Saturday's 3-2 win away at Augsburg," the club said in a statement.

Bayern added a second goal deep in first half stoppage time when Davies rifled in a low shot.

Augsburg bounced back seven minutes after the restart as Ermedin Demirovic was left unmarked in the box and beat keeper Manuel Neuer with a glancing header to cut the deficit.

Kane then tapped in his 23rd league goal in the 58th minute following a lengthy VAR review to restore Bayern's two-goal lead before Augsburg earned an 87th minute penalty.

Neuer picked the right corner and palmed Sven Michel's spot kick wide but Demirovic beat him with another spot kick in stoppage time to inject some late drama into the game.

The defeat leaves Augsburg in 12th place on 21 points.
